confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_d48f8d8e-3698-4d5e-bcc2-7af8ff59438c read back as archived

evidence: Task 75 review closed successfully with verdict accepted_with_notes.
Review findings included a minor note: task commit mutated legacy .ai/agents/roster.json last_done from 79 to 75.
CLI returned capa_recommendation lifecycle_or_roster_authority_mismatch even though the review was accepted, not rejected.
proposed_direction: Separate lifecycle review defects from projection-noise notes so accepted reviews do not emit misleading rejection-language CAPA recommendations.
Clarify whether .ai/agents/roster.json is still an admitted mutable projection; if legacy, stop task finish/review flows from committing it as if authoritative.
Make roster/projection updates monotonic or semantically explicit if lower-number older tasks can be completed after newer tasks.
payload.summary: While reviewing narada-andrey task 75, accepted_with_notes succeeded and closed the task, but review CAPA recommendation triggered from a non-blocking note about .ai/agents/roster.json last_done moving from 79 to 75. This exposes that legacy roster projection remains noisy in governed review closure and can confuse architect loops.
payload.title: Accepted review should not create lifecycle/roster CAPA noise from legacy roster projection

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
